firmwareVersions.raw: Boot Firmware Version: 0x00010000, Main Firmware Version: 0x8001006

certificate.caveat: The module generates SSPs whose strengths are modified by available entropy.
certificate.description: MFP Cryptographic Module(B) is a cryptographic security chip for encrypting data written to a storage device and other security function of Kyocera multifunction printer. Secure key generation and fast AES encryption/decryption are offered through SATA interface. SecureBoot verifies the integrity of the firmware when the product starts up.

services.name: Authentication CO
services.description: This service authorizes CO and loads RAM firmware of the module. ROM Firmware allows this service only, and this service can only run on ROM Firmware. If on RAM Firmware, this service cannot be performed. Integrity of RAM Firmware is verified by using ECDSA verify.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Symmetric Encryption/ Decryption
services.description: This service encrypts or decrypts supplied data using AES-ECB, AES- CBC or AES-CTR.
services.cspsAccessed: AES-ECB Key, AES-CBC Key, AES-CTR Key (128, 192, 256 bits)
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Symmetric Encryption/De cryption with Authentication
services.description: This service encrypts or decrypts supplied data with authentication using AES-GCM Key.
services.cspsAccessed: AES-GCM Key, AES- CCM Key (128, 192, 256 bits)
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Hash
services.description: This service generates a message digest using Message Digest function.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: MAC
services.description: This service generates Message Authentication Code on a supplied data using Keyed Hash function or AES-CMAC Key.
services.cspsAccessed: HMAC Key (160, 224, 256, 384, 512 bits) AES-CMAC Key (128, 192, 256 bits)
services.approvedFunctions: HMAC-SHA-1, HMAC-SHA-224, HMAC-SHA-256, HMAC-SHA-384, HMAC-SHA-512, HMAC-SHA- 512/224, HMAC- SHA-512/256 AES-CMAC
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Random Number Generation
services.description: This service generates a random number using the DRBG generate function. A random number, which is unmodified output by this service, can be used as a Key Derive Key.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: RSA-PKCS #1 v1.5 Sign
services.description: This service generates an RSA-PKCS#1 v1.5 signature on a supplied data.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: RSA-PKCS #1 v1.5 Verify
services.description: This service verifies an RSA-PKCS#1 v1.5 signature on a supplied data with a supplied RSA Public Key.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: RSA-PSS Sign
services.description: This service generates an RSA-PSS signature on a supplied data.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: RSA-PSS Verify
services.description: This service verifies an RSA-PSS signature on a supplied data with a supplied RSA Public Key.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: ECDSA Sign
services.description: This service generates an ECDSA signature on a supplied data.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: ECDSA Verify
services.description: This service verifies an ECDSA signature on a supplied data with supplied ECDSA Public Keys.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and

services.name: ECC CDH Key Agreement
services.description: This service generates Shared Secret with other party’s ECDH Public Key.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: ECC Multiplication
services.description: This service computes ECDSA/ECDH Public Key with ECDSA/ECDH Private Key, which is imported or generated by a corresponding service.
services.cspsAccessed: ECDSA Private Key, ECDH Private Key
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: ECC Public-Key Verify
services.description: This service verifies supplied ECDSA/ECDH Public Key by checking if the following conditions hold. 1. y 2=x 3+ax +b Q Q Q (mod p) 2. nQ=O Here, Q=(x ,y ) is the Q Q ECDSA/ECDH Public Key.
services.indicator: Approved security functions are not used in this service.

services.name: Key Derivation
services.description: This Service generates an AES-ECB Key, an AES-CBC Key, an AES- CTR Key, an AES-GCM Key, a HMAC Key, an AES-CMAC Key, a Key Derive Key, a Key Wrap
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: XTS Key Derivation
services.description: This Service generates an AES-XTS key, which is as concatenation of two AES keys, that are 256 bits long.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.description: If a CSP is Shared Secret generated by ECC CDH Key Agreement service, this service outputs Shared Secret in plaintext by 2-step procedure.
services.indicator: command result.
services.name: If a CSP is Shared Secret generated by ECC CDH Key Agreement service, this service outputs Shared Secret in plaintext by 2-step procedure.
Delete Key
services.description: This service zeroises a CSP in the SRAM.
services.cspsAccessed: AES Key, HMAC Key, RSA Private Key, ECC Private Key, Key Derive Key, Key Wrap Key
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Clear OTP
services.description: This service zeroises a CSP in the OTP.
services.cspsAccessed: AES Key, HMAC Key, ECC Private Key, Key Derive Key, Key Wrap Key
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Delete All Keys
services.description: This service zeroises All CSPs in the SRAM, the XTS-shell and the OTP.
services.cspsAccessed: AES Key, HMAC Key, RSA Private Key, ECC Private Key, Key Derive Key, Key Wrap Key, Crypto Officer Password, User Password
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Random Number Generator Configuration
services.description: This service gives configuration parameters of ENT and does the DRBG instantiate function.
services.cspsAccessed: DRBG Entropy Input, DRBG Nonce Input, DRBG Internal State
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Self-Test
services.description: This service performs Self-Tests described in Chapter 9. This service is invoked automatically at power-up of the module. This service is not provided as command via the mailbox. This service does not include AES-XTS self- test, that is performed by invoking XTS-shell Enable service.
services.approvedFunctions: AES-ECB, AES- CBC, ECDSA verify (SHA-256) (The followings only run on RAM Firmware.) AES-CMAC SHA-256 RSA, ECC CDH, ECDSA sign
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Monotonic Counter Increment
services.description: This service increments the monotonic counter in the OTP. The monotonic counter is stored in plaintext. The monotonic counter is implemented to support a firmware rollback prevention. The processing system can detect a firmware rollback by making reference to it.

services.name: Monotonic Counter Read
services.description: This service returns the value of the monotonic counter in the OTP in plaintext.

services.name: Write OTP
services.description: This service performs a write operation of a Key, a Hash Digest or User Defined Data into the OTP in plaintext.
services.cspsAccessed: AES-Key HMAC-Key ECC-Private Key Key-Derive Key, Key- Wrap Key
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: Read OTP
services.description: This service performs a read operation of the User Defined Data from the OTP in plaintext.

services.name: Register User
services.description: This service registers a user who is assigned USER role. Both User ID and User Password are set to the SRAM.

services.name: XTS-shell Enable
services.description: This service enables the XTS-shell by releasing the reset, starts AES-XTS self- test. And then self-test completes successfully, AES-XTS keys which are in the SRAM are set into registers of XTS- shell.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

services.name: XTS-shell Disable
services.description: This service deletes AES-XTS keys in the XTS-shell and disables
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using

services.description: the XTS-shell by applying the reset.
services.indicator: both CFG-ID service and command result.
services.name: the XTS-shell by applying the reset.
AES-XTS Encryption/ Decryption
services.description: This service uses AES- XTS to encrypt or decrypt data which is received via XTS-shell IF. It only can run while XTS-shell is enabled.
services.roles: As XTS-shell IF is a responder port, the source which transfers data to XTS-shell can’t be identified. Therefore, this service does not require authentication.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using CFG-ID service.

services.name: Status Check
services.description: This service returns the status of the module.

services.name: Version Check
services.description: This service returns the hardware and the firmware version of the module.

services.name: CFG-ID Check
services.description: This service returns the value of configuration ID by reading the register. If the return value lower 3 bits of this service is 0x2, it indicates that the module is approved mode.

services.name: Sleep
services.description: This service deletes AES-XTS keys in the XTS-shell and disables the XTS-shell by applying the reset, and causes the module to transition the WAIT FOR SLEEP state. The WAIT FOR SLEEP state is the state to wait for applying the sleep reset.
services.indicator: Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

DRBG SP800-90A

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: If Key Transport function is used, the module generates 96-bits AES-GCM Encryption IV which is random bits generated by the approved HASH_DRBG. The random number from ENT which the module holds is used as the entropy input of HASH_DRBG.

If Symmetric Encryption and Decryption with Authentication function is used, AES-GCM Encryption IV is generated and provided from inside of the single chip but outside of the module. The IV length is 96-bits. This IV must be generated by the GCM IV generation method in accordance with FIPS 140-3 IG C.H.

rows: MFP Cryptographic Module (B) as a sub-chip cryptographic subsystem
AES SP 800-38C SP 800-38D FIPS 140-3 IG C.H
HMAC-SHA-1, HMAC- SHA-224, HMAC-SHA- 256, HMAC-SHA-384, HMAC-SHA-512, HMAC-SHA-512/224, HMAC-SHA-512/256
Key Size HMAC-SHA-1 : 160bits HMAC-SHA-224 : 224 bits HMAC-SHA-256 : 256 bits HMAC-SHA-384 : 384 bits HMAC-SHA-512, HMAC-SHA- 512/224, HMAC-SHA-512/256 : 512 bits
Key Agreement Scheme Shared Secret Computation
Elliptic Curve Cryptography Key Pair Generation
Validation of an elliptic curve public key.
KTS (AES Key Wrapping with AES- GCM) FIP 197 SP800-38D FIPS 140-3 IG C.H FIPS 140-3 IG D.G
Export Key service RSA-PSS Sign service ECDSA Sign service Random Number Generation service Key Derivation service

If Key Transport function is used, the module generates 96-bits AES-GCM Encryption IV which is random bits generated by the approved HASH_DRBG. The random number from ENT which the module holds is used as the entropy input of HASH_DRBG.

If Symmetric Encryption and Decryption with Authentication function is used, AES-GCM Encryption IV is generated and provided from inside of the single chip but outside of the module. The IV length is 96-bits. This IV must be generated by the GCM IV generation method in accordance with FIPS 140-3 IG C.H.

256-bit ECDSA signature verification (only for Authentication CO service)

A 256-bit ECDSA signature has 128 bits of security. The probability of signature that a single random authentication attempt will succeed, or a false acceptance will occur is 1/2128 which is less than 1/1,000,000. When the attempt fails, the module waits at least one second, during which any attempt is ignored. Thus, the maximum authentication rate is 60 per minute and the probability that random authentication attempts will succeed within a one-minute interval is 60/2128 which is less than 1/100,000.

The probability of password that a single random authentication attempt (by guessing the password value) will succeed, or a false acceptance will occur is 1/232 which is less than 1/1,000,000. When the attempt fails, the module waits at least one second, during which any attempt is ignored. Thus, the maximum authentication rate is 60 per minute and the probability that random authentication attempts will succeed within a one-minute interval is 60/232 which is less than 1/100,000.

This service authorizes CO and loads RAM firmware of the module. ROM Firmware allows this service only, and this service can only run on ROM Firmware. If on RAM Firmware, this service cannot be performed. Integrity of RAM Firmware is verified by using ECDSA verify.
Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.
This service encrypts or decrypts supplied data using AES-ECB, AES- CBC or AES-CTR.
AES-ECB Key, AES-CBC Key, AES-CTR Key (128, 192, 256 bits)
Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.
This service encrypts or decrypts supplied data with authentication using AES-GCM Key.

This service generates a random number using the DRBG generate function. A random number, which is unmodified output by this service, can be used as a Key Derive Key.

This service verifies supplied ECDSA/ECDH Public Key by checking if the following conditions hold. 1. y 2=x 3+ax +b Q Q Q (mod p) 2. nQ=O Here, Q=(x ,y ) is the Q Q ECDSA/ECDH Public Key.
Approved security functions are not used in this service.

This Service generates an AES-XTS key, which is as concatenation of two AES keys, that are 256 bits long.
Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

This service stores a key supplied from outside of the module into the SRAM inside the module. This service can also be used to copy a Key in the OTP into the SRAM. If a supplied key is encrypted by key-wrap algorithm, which is AES-GCM, the supplied key is stored into the SRAM after key- unwrapping.
Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.
This service encrypts a CSP stored in the SRAM with key-wrap algorithm, which is AES-GCM, and outputs the encrypted CSP.
Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and
If a CSP is Shared Secret generated by ECC CDH Key Agreement service, this service outputs Shared Secret in plaintext by 2-step procedure.
This service zeroises a CSP in the SRAM.

This service performs Self-Tests described in Chapter 9. This service is invoked automatically at power-up of the module. This service is not provided as command via the mailbox. This service does not include AES-XTS self- test, that is performed by invoking XTS-shell Enable service.
AES-ECB, AES- CBC, ECDSA verify (SHA-256) (The followings only run on RAM Firmware.) AES-CMAC SHA-256 RSA, ECC CDH, ECDSA sign
Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.

This service increments the monotonic counter in the OTP. The monotonic counter is stored in plaintext. The monotonic counter is implemented to support a firmware rollback prevention. The processing system can detect a firmware rollback by making reference to it.
This service returns the value of the monotonic counter in the OTP in plaintext.
This service performs a write operation of a Key, a Hash Digest or User Defined Data into the OTP in plaintext.
AES-Key HMAC-Key ECC-Private Key Key-Derive Key, Key- Wrap Key
Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.
This service performs a read operation of the User Defined Data from the OTP in plaintext.
This service registers a user who is assigned USER role. Both User ID and User Password are set to the SRAM.

This service enables the XTS-shell by releasing the reset, starts AES-XTS self- test. And then self-test completes successfully, AES-XTS keys which are in the SRAM are set into registers of XTS- shell.
Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.
This service deletes AES-XTS keys in the XTS-shell and disables
Indicator can be checked by using
the XTS-shell by applying the reset.
both CFG-ID service and command result.
This service uses AES- XTS to encrypt or decrypt data which is received via XTS-shell IF. It only can run while XTS-shell is enabled.
As XTS-shell IF is a responder port, the source which transfers data to XTS-shell can’t be identified. Therefore, this service does not require authentication.
Indicator can be checked by using CFG-ID service.
This service returns the status of the module.
This service returns the hardware and the firmware version of the module.
This service returns the value of configuration ID by reading the register. If the return value lower 3 bits of this service is 0x2, it indicates that the module is approved mode.

This service deletes AES-XTS keys in the XTS-shell and disables the XTS-shell by applying the reset, and causes the module to transition the WAIT FOR SLEEP state. The WAIT FOR SLEEP state is the state to wait for applying the sleep reset.
Indicator can be checked by using both CFG-ID service and command result.
Each time a service person performs maintenance or collects the board in the event of some kind of faults occurring on the machine that SCH134 is mounted.
